purpose: Portable handoff context for continuing the Yamaha FB-01 editor work after Codex sidebar navigation opened a blank project context.

originalTask.title: Research FB-01 SysEx
originalTask.note: The old task is still readable through the task API, but opening it from the app appeared to route to a fresh/blank project composer. The actual repo is nested under the recorded cwd.

researchSummary.feasibility: A Yamaha FB-01 voice/config editor for macOS is feasible. The device exposes SysEx parameter changes, dump requests, bulk dumps, store commands, and unit/config/voice data flows that are enough for an editor/librarian.
researchSummary.transportPlan: Use CoreMIDI on macOS after the offline SysEx model is reliable.
importantSafetyNotes: Start with receive/capture and parsing before any write/send features.
Handle Yamaha checksums and voice-data nibble packing/unpacking correctly.
Treat memory-protect, throttled SysEx sending, explicit slot selection, compare/confirm, and read-back-after-write as required for safe writes.
The FB-01 has an 8-instrument configuration model, not just a single-patch workflow.
sourceMemory: Manualzz OCR of the owner manual
VOGONS service-manual-derived command map by Scali

placeholders.lo_hi: split 8-bit value as two 4-bit MIDI-safe nibbles

instrumentMessages.purpose: Store current instrument voice into voice RAM

systemAndConfigurationMessages.purpose: Each voice bank bulk dump request

encodingNotes: Voice data uses two-byte nibble pairs because some internal voice bytes use the high bit.
Configuration data can be sent as normal 7-bit bytes because its format does not set the high bit.

One parser ambiguity encountered: byte 0C can mean voice bank bulk or instrument 4 voice depending on the rest of the frame; current code prefers the bank shape when the extra bank byte and packet stream are present.

implementedMilestones.summary: Created a Swift package with typed FB-01 SysEx command builders, Yamaha checksum helpers, MIDI-safe nibble packing/unpacking, early voice/config/bulk packet containers, and byte-level tests.
implementedMilestones.verification: swift test passed with 7 tests before the repo rename.

implementedMilestones.summary: Renamed /Users/ricercar/Documents/GitHub/FB01-editor to /Users/ricercar/Documents/GitHub/FB01Editor and normalized Package.swift package name to FB01Editor.
implementedMilestones.verification: Cleared stale .build cache and swift test passed with 7 tests from the new path.

implementedMilestones.summary: Added incoming SysEx parsing/classification, file-level artifact modeling, and tests for separate voice-bank/config artifacts plus .syx file round-trip.
Committed the offline core baseline before hardware testing.
implementedMilestones.verification: Commit be51aa5 Initial FB-01 SysEx core; worktree clean; swift test passed with 13 tests immediately before commit.

designDecisions: Build an offline editor/librarian core before requiring the FB-01 hardware.
Treat single voices, voice banks, current configurations, stored configurations, configuration sets, unit ID dumps, and raw SysEx as distinct artifact kinds.
Plan file handling so voice banks and configurations can be saved as separate files, avoiding a monolithic document model.
Use a layered parser: reliable SysEx splitter/classifier first, then packet decoding for dump families that can be safely identified.
Delay write-back until read/capture/import/export are trustworthy.

nextRecommendedStep.title: Add a hardware-safe CoreMIDI capture path
nextRecommendedStep.summary: Add a small CoreMIDI layer and command-line executable target, tentatively fb01-dump, before adding any write/send features.
tasks: Add a CoreMIDI wrapper that lists MIDI sources and destinations.
Add an executable target, likely fb01-dump.
Save received SysEx messages as .syx files.
Parse/classify received dumps using the existing FB01Editor core.
Test by using the FB-01 front panel to manually send a bulk dump into the Mac.
Only after capture works, add request commands from the Mac.
Only after read-back is trustworthy, add guarded write-back.

importPrompt: Continue the Yamaha FB-01 editor work in /Users/ricercar/Documents/GitHub/FB01Editor. Use this JSON as recovered context. The repo is a Swift package on main at commit be51aa5 (Initial FB-01 SysEx core), with a clean worktree and 13 passing tests at the last verified point. The next step is to add a hardware-safe CoreMIDI capture path: a CLI target like fb01-dump that can list MIDI devices, listen for incoming SysEx, save .syx files, and parse/classify dumps using the existing core. Do not add write/send features until receive/capture and read-back are trustworthy.
